abstract: A Boolean value indicating whether the counterpart app is available for live messaging.
overview:
## Discussion

This property is [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/true] when the WatchKit extension and the iOS app can communicate with each other.

Specifically: 

In all other cases, the value is [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/false].

The counterpart must be reachable in order for you to send messages using the [doc://com.apple.watchconnectivity/documentation/WatchConnectivity/WCSession/sendMessage(_:replyHandler:errorHandler:)] and [doc://com.apple.watchconnectivity/documentation/WatchConnectivity/WCSession/sendMessageData(_:replyHandler:errorHandler:)] methods. Sending messages to a counterpart that is not reachable results in an error.

The value in this property is valid only for a configured session that has been activated successfully. If the [doc://com.apple.watchconnectivity/documentation/WatchConnectivity/WCSession/activationState] property is available, its value must be [doc://com.apple.watchconnectivity/documentation/WatchConnectivity/WCSessionActivationState/activated]. When the session becomes inactive or deactivated, you should ignore the value in this property.

# isReachable

**Instance Property**

A Boolean value indicating whether the counterpart app is available for live messaging.

## Declaration

```swift
var isReachable: Bool { get }
```

## Discussion

This property is [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/true] when the WatchKit extension and the iOS app can communicate with each other.

Specifically: 

- **WatchKit extension.** The iOS device is within range, so communication can occur and the WatchKit extension is running in the foreground, or is running with a high priority in the background (for example, during a workout session or when a complication is loading its initial timeline data).
- **iOS app.** A paired and active Apple Watch is in range, the corresponding WatchKit extension is running, and the WatchKit extension’s [doc://com.apple.watchconnectivity/documentation/WatchConnectivity/WCSession/isReachable] property is [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/true].

In all other cases, the value is [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/false].

The counterpart must be reachable in order for you to send messages using the [doc://com.apple.watchconnectivity/documentation/WatchConnectivity/WCSession/sendMessage(_:replyHandler:errorHandler:)] and [doc://com.apple.watchconnectivity/documentation/WatchConnectivity/WCSession/sendMessageData(_:replyHandler:errorHandler:)] methods. Sending messages to a counterpart that is not reachable results in an error.

The value in this property is valid only for a configured session that has been activated successfully. If the [doc://com.apple.watchconnectivity/documentation/WatchConnectivity/WCSession/activationState] property is available, its value must be [doc://com.apple.watchconnectivity/documentation/WatchConnectivity/WCSessionActivationState/activated]. When the session becomes inactive or deactivated, you should ignore the value in this property.
title: isReachable
